summary: Gruber examines Google Analytics data for Daring Fireball to reveal the iPhone 6 vs. 6 Plus adoption split among his readers. The data shows a roughly 3-to-1 ratio favoring the iPhone 6 over the 6 Plus, consistent with broader industry reports, though supply constraints on the Plus may skew results. He notes that iPhone 6 models already outnumber all iPhone 5-era devices combined among his readership. Comparing to the iPhone 5 launch in 2012, adoption rates are similar, suggesting DF readers consistently upgrade early. The broader trend shows mobile traffic growth driven almost entirely by iPhones, with iPads flat. OS stats confirm DF's audience is overwhelmingly Apple-centric, with Android at just 2.1%.
key_points: The iPhone 6 outsells the 6 Plus roughly 3-to-1 among DF readers, consistent with wider reports, though 6 Plus supply constraints may be a factor
iPhone 6 models already outnumber all iPhone 5/5S/5C devices combined just weeks after launch, showing rapid adoption by DF's audience
Mobile traffic to DF grew from 32.7% to 44% over two years, with nearly all growth coming from iPhones while iPad share stayed flat
DF's OS breakdown reveals an overwhelmingly Apple audience: 83% iOS+Mac, with Android at 2.1% and Windows Phone at 0.2%
DF readers are likely unrepresentative of the general public, being early adopters who buy new iPhones at launch
money_quotes: There are already slightly more DF readers using one of the iPhones 6 than the iPhone 5/5C/5S combined. You're my people.
I suspect DF readers have always been the type of people who buy new iPhones as soon as they come out.
The growth in mobile usage among DF readers over the last two years is almost entirely from the iPhone.
key_insight: Daring Fireball's analytics serve as a window into enthusiast Apple adoption patterns, showing a 3-to-1 iPhone 6 vs. 6 Plus split and mobile growth driven almost entirely by iPhones.
